description: Declarative mode registry: the single source of truth for the discriminator (workflowMode/packetKind/backendKind) PLUS the advisorRouting projection for mcp-tooling. The hub reads this file to resolve a mode; the advisor routes any MCP tool-bridge query to the single identity mcp-tooling and the hub picks the mode. All three modes route by hub membership (routingClass 'metadata'), so there is no advisor map entry per mode and no merged-identity projection — the advisor does not read this file at runtime.

discriminator.workflowMode: Public hub/mode key. Stable identity used by the hub router and this registry. Carried by every mode.
discriminator.packetKind: The axis discriminator: 'workflow' for the two local-mutating tool bridges (mcp-chrome-devtools, mcp-click-up) that write to this repo's workspace; 'transport' for mcp-figma, which bridges to Figma Desktop rather than performing design judgment or mutating this workspace.
discriminator.backendKind: Which backend runs the mode: cli-plus-mcp for the workflow bridges (CLI-primary, Code Mode MCP fallback); figma-desktop-transport for the figma transport (drives the external Figma Desktop app over its local daemon).

advisorRoutingContract.routingClass: How the advisor finds the mode. For mcp-tooling every mode is 'metadata' = resolved by hub membership; the advisor routes the single identity mcp-tooling and the hub picks the mode, so no mode carries an advisor map entry or a merged-identity projection.
advisorRoutingContract.packetSkillName: The packet's SKILL.md name. For mcp-tooling the packet folder IS its packetSkillName (folder mcp-chrome-devtools == packetSkillName mcp-chrome-devtools, and likewise for mcp-click-up and mcp-figma) — so no mode has a folder/name mismatch and grandfatheredFolderMismatch is false throughout.
advisorRoutingContract.grandfatheredFolderMismatch: true only when a packet folder differs from its packetSkillName (a preserved legacy mismatch). All three mcp-tooling modes use folder == packetSkillName, so none has a mismatch — grandfatheredFolderMismatch is false for all three.
advisorRoutingContract.command: The bound slash command for the mode. None of the three mcp-tooling modes has a dedicated command today (command:null on all three, per ADR-006); the /doctor:mcp router is a separate command that covers install/debug for every mcp-* skill, including mcp-tooling's three members.

transport-axis.description: Declares the TRANSPORT axis. packetKind:'transport' entries bridge to an external tool's CLI/MCP surface (backendKind:'figma-desktop-transport', routingClass:'metadata', mutatesWorkspace:false since writes land in Figma Desktop, not this repo). mcp-figma never performs design judgment itself and carries a mandatory cross-hub pairing to sk-design (a DIFFERENT hub) for design decisions — licensed by ADR-002 as a legitimate cross-hub judgment partner, since neither of mcp-tooling's own workflow modes (browser inspection, task management) can supply design judgment.
